# The Way In

> A free site that welcomes complete beginners — teenagers to retirees, zero technical background — into agentic AI coding tools. Its message: if you can write a good chat message, you already have the core skill; an agent is an AI that doesn't just answer but does things on your computer, always asking permission first.

## Pages

- [The Way In](./): the main single-scroll funnel — chat vs agent, a scripted test drive, fear-calming, tool picker, first prompts
- [The Prompt Pantry](./prompts/): 22 paste-ready prompts organized by life situation, plus a build-your-own Prompt Mixer
- [The Mission Deck](./missions/): 8 scripted, zero-risk agent test drives to play through
- [Your First Day](./first-day/): an honest 10-moment walkthrough of a first real session, including errors and coming back tomorrow
- [It Asked Me Something Weird](./help/): a decoder for confusing moments, plus a plain-words dictionary of every term used

## What's on the main page

- Chat vs. agent, explained side by side with the same request
- A fully scripted in-browser "test drive" of an agent session (pure theater — nothing executes; the visitor sends a pre-written message and personally grants or denies permission)
- Honest fear-calming: agents ask first, practice happens in an empty folder, worst case you delete one folder
- A tool picker quiz and four tool cards (see Tools below)
- Three complete copy-paste starter prompts: a personal "about me" page, a tiny presentation site, and a small decision-wheel web app

## Tools

- [Claude Code](https://claude.com/product/claude-code): Anthropic's coding agent; app or terminal; included in Claude Pro
- [Claude Code docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview): official documentation
- [OpenAI Codex](https://developers.openai.com/codex): OpenAI's coding agent; inside ChatGPT or the terminal; included in ChatGPT Plus
- [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai): free, open-source terminal coding agent; works with an existing Claude/ChatGPT subscription or your own API keys
- [Hermes Agent](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com): free, open-source, self-hosted, by Nous Research; for when you're ready for more

## Further reading

- [Claude](https://claude.ai): the chat assistant many visitors already use
- [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com): likewise
- [MDN command line crash course](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn_web_development/Getting_started/Environment_setup/Command_line): a gentle explainer of what the terminal really is

## Notes

- Static site, no accounts, no tracking, nothing for sale.
- The in-page "agent session" is scripted demonstration data, not a real agent.
